novel_features.name: Cross-venue topic bundle
novel_features.description: Get every related Polymarket and Kalshi market for a topic in one slim ranked ~3KB bundle — kanye-west, argentina, chatgpt-5.
novel_features.rationale: Local FTS5 over both venues' synced markets + events + tags lets us rank cross-venue without server-side support; Polymarket /public-search ranks closed markets alongside active and Kalshi has no full-text search at all.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs current odds on a topic, this is the one-call answer across both venues without fanning out to two platform tools and re-ranking by hand.

novel_features.name: Mispriced screen
novel_features.description: Find same-outcome markets where Polymarket and Kalshi disagree on implied probability by more than a threshold.
novel_features.rationale: Requires PM-to-Kalshi outcome pairing in a local store; no API offers this cross-venue spread.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The clearest signal that one venue is wrong or one side is mispricing — useful for calibration research, not trading.

novel_features.name: Cross-venue compare
novel_features.description: Side-by-side YES/NO and implied probability for the same topic across Polymarket and Kalshi.
novel_features.rationale: No upstream API returns paired same-outcome markets across venues; the local store resolves topic-to-pair via FTS5 + topic-text overlap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells an agent or analyst 'which venue has the better/different number on this outcome' in one read-only call.

novel_features.name: Trending screen
novel_features.description: Top movers by 24h volume across both venues, ranked.
novel_features.rationale: Neither venue exposes a unified trending feed; the local store ranks across both by 24h volume.
novel_features.why_it_matters: One command answers 'what should I be watching today' without scraping two homepages.

novel_features.name: Resolving screen
novel_features.description: Markets resolving in the next week/month/days, sorted by liquidity.
novel_features.rationale: PM has end-date filters, Kalshi doesn't expose them cleanly; unification only possible locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells an agent 'what's about to settle' without re-paging two cursors.

novel_features.name: Liquid screen
novel_features.description: Markets above a normalized volume/liquidity floor across both venues.
novel_features.rationale: PM has liquidity_num_min; Kalshi doesn't — normalizing across venues only works locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Filters out thin markets that will move on a single 100-dollar bet.

novel_features.name: Movers screen
novel_features.description: Biggest implied-probability deltas over a 24h or 7d window across both venues.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs current price vs price-history snapshot; requires synced history; no API call returns this directly.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces narrative shifts (price-driven) vs hype shifts (volume-driven from ).

novel_features.name: New screen
novel_features.description: Markets created in the last N days across both venues.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-venue unification of created-at filtering; PM and Kalshi disagree on how to expose it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Newly listed markets are where the alpha and mispricings live.

novel_features.name: Markets diff
novel_features.description: Field-by-field structural diff between a specific Polymarket market and a specific Kalshi market.
novel_features.rationale: Local join across two different schemas; no upstream tool does cross-schema diff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you already know the two slugs/tickers (e.g. from `topic <theme>`), diff shows you exactly where the venues disagree.

narrative.headline: Every Polymarket and Kalshi market in one slim agent-native CLI, with cross-venue topic search and screens no other tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Read-only by design and by CI lint — the binary structurally cannot trade. `topic <name>` returns every related Polymarket + Kalshi market/event/tag in one ~3KB ranked bundle (vs the official Polymarket CLI's ~250KB firehose). Local SQLite + FTS5 keeps queries instant and free after one sync. Six screens (`trending`, `resolving`, `liquid`, `mispriced`, `movers`, `new`) cover the workflows agents and odds researchers run every week.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. Both Polymarket Gamma and Kalshi v2 trade-api public endpoints work without a key for everything this CLI does (markets, events, tags, series, orderbooks, prices). The binary contains no signing code, no wallet handling, and no order-placement endpoints — CI lint enforces this on every PR.

quickstart.comment: One-time pull of Polymarket and Kalshi market/event/tag data into local SQLite (~30MB, no API key).
The killer feature: every related market across both venues, ranked, slim by default.
Top 24h volume across both Polymarket and Kalshi in one call.
Side-by-side implied probability on the same topic across venues.
Markets settling within the next 7 days, sorted by liquidity.
Same-outcome markets where Polymarket and Kalshi disagree by 5pp or more.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `prediction-goat-pp-cli sync` first. Topic search reads the local SQLite store — without a sync, FTS5 has nothing to rank.

troubleshoots.symptom: `mispriced` or `compare` returns fewer pairs than expected
troubleshoots.fix: The PM-to-Kalshi outcome pairing relies on topic-text overlap; some markets simply don't have a counterpart on the other venue. Try `topic <name> --json` first to see what's available.

troubleshoots.symptom: Polymarket prices look stale after sync
troubleshoots.fix: Re-run `sync --incremental` — Polymarket markets refresh in real time but the local mirror is a snapshot; the sync command refreshes only changed rows.

troubleshoots.symptom: Kalshi endpoint returns empty events list
troubleshoots.fix: Set `--venue polymarket` on the failing command to confirm it's a Kalshi-side reachability issue, then check `prediction-goat-pp-cli doctor --json`.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for prediction-goat-pp-cli when an agent needs current prediction-market odds across both Polymarket and Kalshi without trading. The killer commands are `topic`, `compare`, and the six screens — every other command exists so power users can drill into one venue or one market. The CLI is read-only by structural CI guarantee: it cannot place orders, hold a wallet, or sign trades, which makes it safe to embed in agent toolchains.

recipes.title: Find every market for a topic across both venues
recipes.explanation: Slim ranked bundle of every related PM + Kalshi market. `--select` reduces to four fields per row; an agent sees ~1KB instead of the firehose.

recipes.title: What's settling this week?
recipes.explanation: Local SQL filter on end_date < now+7d across both venues, sorted by liquidity descending. `--select` keeps the response tiny.

recipes.title: Side-by-side odds comparison
recipes.explanation: Resolves the topic to paired markets and renders YES/NO + implied prob for each venue side-by-side. Tells you exactly where PM and Kalshi disagree.

recipes.title: Catch mispricings across venues
recipes.explanation: Returns same-outcome market pairs where implied probabilities diverge by 5+ percentage points. Slim output via `--select`.
